title: The Art of Shedding: Spring Claw Care
description: As spring progresses, learn how to help your cat manage seasonal claw shedding and maintain healthy paws.
main_content: Spring is a time of renewal, and for your feline friend, that means shedding old claw sheaths to make way for sharp new ones. This natural process can leave claw husks around your home, but it's also an opportunity to check paw health. To assist: provide sturdy scratching posts (sisal or cardboard), offer omega-3 supplements (consult vet), and gently massage paws to stimulate circulation. Avoid pulling off loose sheaths—let them fall naturally. Watch for overgrown claws that may need trimming, especially in senior cats. A balanced diet rich in biotin and zinc supports claw strength.
key_takeaways: Spring shedding is normal; provide scratching surfaces.
Never pull off loose claw sheaths.
Supplement diet with omega-3s for healthy claws.
Check for overgrown claws requiring trimming.
